lines.en_lit: Rabbi Yitzḥak Nappaḥa raised an objection to Rabbi Ami: “And he shall remove the entire bull that he should remove the entire

“And the bull of the sin-offering and the goat of the sin-offering, Rav Pappa said: Everyone agrees that with its hide, its flesh, and its excrement, When they disagree to blood. One Sage holds blood is called a bull, and one Sage holds blood is not called a bull.

Rav Ashi said: It is reasonable in accordance with the one who said blood is called bull, as it is written: “With this Aaron shall come into the sacred place, with a bull” Is that to say he brings in, with its horns? Rather, with its blood, and calls that “a bull.”
And the other one, With what did Aaron become qualified to enter the sacred place? With a young bull for a sin-offering.

And let him derive that it is a sin-offering whose owners have died. a sin-offering whose owners have died is left to die, Ravin bar Rav Adda said to Rava: Your students say Rav Amram said is a communal sin-offering, is not left to die.

As we learned Rabbi Meir said to him: But the Yom Kippur bull, and the meal-offering resembling a wafer High Priest, and Paschal offering, is an individual offering and overrides Shabbat and ritual impurity. not by inference that there is one who says that communal?

And according to your reasoning, that taught Rabbi Ya’akov said to him: But bull for an unwitting communal sin, and the goats for idolatry, Festival peace-offering, which communal offerings and override neither Shabbat nor ritual impurity. by inference that there is one who says that individual

Rather, responded to the first tanna, as he heard him say Communal sacrifices override Shabbat and ritual impurity, but individual sacrifices override neither Shabbat nor ritual impurity. Rabbi Meir said to him: an individual offering principle? But the Yom Kippur bull, and the meal-offering resembling a wafer High Priest, and Paschal offering, is an individual offering and overrides Shabbat and ritual impurity.

And Rabbi Ya’akov responded to a communal offering principle, But bull for an unwitting communal sin, and the goats for idolatry, Festival peace-offering, which communal offerings and override neither Shabbat nor ritual impurity.

Rather, grasp this principle: Any that a fixed time overrides Shabbat and ritual impurity even if individual and any of no fixed time overrides neither Shabbat nor ritual impurity, and even is a communal

Abaye raised an objection to bull and goat of Yom Kippur that were lost, and he separated others in their stead, all shall die. And likewise, goats for idolatry that were lost and he separated others in their stead, all of them shall die. the statement of Rabbi Yehuda. Rabbi Elazar and Rabbi Shimon say: They shall graze until they become unfit, they are sold and their proceeds go free-will offering, as a communal sin-offering is not die.
said to What bull bull for an unwitting communal sin. But taught: Of Yom Kippur, When taught: to the goat.

But wasn’t it taught the bull of Yom Kippur and the goat of Yom Kippur that were lost and he separated others in their stead, all shall die. the statement of Rabbi Yehuda. Rabbi Elazar and Rabbi Shimon say: They shall graze until they become unfit, they are sold and their proceeds go free-will offering, as a communal sin-offering is not die.
Do not say: As a communal sin-offering is not die. Rather, say: As a sin-offering of partners is not die. And what is practical difference
that priests do not bring a bull for ruling,
Come hear, as Rabbi Elazar raised dilemma:
